conditional_states.condition: Multi-file or resumable upload where some files succeed and others are still in progress or failed.
Uploading is blocked by quota, file-type, or current workflow step.
Upload must pause and resume or queue when connectivity drops mid-transfer.
Large files require a determinate progress indicator and time/percentage feedback.
User lacks rights to upload to the target location.
Drag-and-drop must have a tap-to-browse equivalent on coarse-pointer devices.

rationale: Uploads are long-running, fallible transfers, so determinate loading (progress), explicit per-file error with retry, and a clear success confirmation are mandatory. The drop zone needs hover/active/focus affordances and a keyboard-accessible file picker because drag-and-drop alone is not operable by keyboard or assistive tech.
evidence: WCAG 2.2 2.1.1 (Keyboard): drag-and-drop must have a keyboard-operable alternative.
WCAG 2.2 4.1.3 (Status Messages): upload progress and completion must be announced.
NN/g and platform guidelines: show determinate progress, per-file status, and a retry path for failed uploads.
